title: 10 best herbal tinctures for swollen gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a unique compound called calendula, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. When taken as a tincture, calendula works to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the swollen gums, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate swelling in the gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold can help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting healing.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections in the gums that may be contributing to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.

Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers in full bloom and clean them thoroughly by hand or using a gentle stream of water.
Combine one part fresh calendula flowers with two parts 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar, covering the contents completely.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least four weeks to allow the herbs to infuse into the solvent, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture liquid.
Transfer the tincture liquid to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, store them in the refrigerator and use within two years, taking 20-30 drops as needed for swollen gums three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease pain. The tincture's active compounds, such as salvianolic acid B, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation in the gums. Additionally, danshen's antioxidant properties help protect the gum tissue from oxidative damage, promoting a healthy environment that fosters healing and reduces bleeding. This natural remedy provides relief for individuals experiencing swollen, tender gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial property of Danshen helps combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum inflammation and disease, reducing the severity of swollen gums and preventing further infection.
Antioxidant property: Danshen's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and exacerbate swelling, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Danshen plant contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Danshen possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate gum swelling and prevent further inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia miltiorrhiza roots in a dark room or airtight container.
Grind the roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix one part powdered root with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for eight weeks shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean dropper bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with swollen gums because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and soothe gum tissue. The tannins and flavonoids present in licorice root have potent antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat inflammation caused by bacteria, plaque, and other factors. By reducing inflammation, herbal licorice tinctures can alleviate pain, discomfort, and redness associated with swollen gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice root tincture contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Antibacterial: The tannins present in licorice root tincture have antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation.

Soothing and calming: Licorice root tincture has a sweet, soothing quality that can calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth and reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like isoflavones and phenolic acids, which help alleviate gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice root glycyrrhiza glabra and chop it into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped licorice root with four parts 190-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks allowing maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The tincture's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and terpenes, work to combat inflammation by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum swelling. By using herbal kansas coneflower tinctures, individuals can promote a healthy oral environment and reduce the risk of further gum complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and chicoric acid, which may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Antimicrobial properties: The tincture's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to swollen gums, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower is known for its immune-boosting properties, which may help stimulate the body's natural defenses against infection and reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective at reducing inflammation and swelling in gums.
Seeds: Containing saponins and other bioactive compounds, seeds have been traditionally used to reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots, measuring one part roots to two parts vodka by weight.
Chop the roots into small pieces and transfer them to a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Add 80 proof vodka to cover the roots completely, ensuring at least a 1:2 root-to-liquid ratio remains.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction processes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The natural antioxidants present in dandelion root help to combat inflammation-causing free radicals, thereby alleviating gum inflammation. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in dandelion tincture work to eliminate bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting healthy gums and a reduced risk of swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial components of dandelion tincture, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, combat bacterial overgrowth on the gum line, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation in the gums, thereby promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves from a pesticide-free area weighing at least 100 grams.
Clean and chop the harvested dandelion material into small pieces ensuring proper air circulation.
Combine the chopped dandelion with 80% to 95% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar covering it completely.
Allow the mixture to macerate for at least two weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer it to dark glass bottles storing them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Berberine, a key compound found in goldenseal, has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting enzymes that break down collagen, thereby reducing gum tissue damage. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections, which can contribute to gum inflammation. By taking an herbal goldenseal tincture orally or applying it topically to the affected area, individuals may experience relief from swollen gums and a reduced risk of further infection.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A key alkaloid in goldenseal, berberine has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease gum pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids (IAs): Goldenseal's IAs have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections contributing to swollen gums, such as gingivitis and periodontitis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds in goldenseal tinctures exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help reduce gum inflammation and promote healing by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in berberine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain.
Barks: The barks contain alkaloids like berberine and hygrine, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh goldenseal roots or purchase dried root material from a reputable supplier.
Cleanly wash the dried root material with warm water to remove impurities and debris.
Chop the cleaned root material into small pieces and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Combine the chopped root material with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts solvent and steep for 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as apiol and myristicin, work to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, sweet cicely has antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections and promote a healthy oral environment. As a result, regular use of sweet cicely tinctures can effectively alleviate swollen and painful gums, providing natural relief from discomfort and promoting overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Sweet cicely tincture has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum inflammation and infection, thereby reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce pain and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a hormone that promotes inflammation.

Antioxidant: Sweet cicely tincture is rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and exacerbate gum inflammation, thereby promoting healing and reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet cicely roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks of sweet cicely contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent further irritation, soothing swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sweet cicely leaves and flowers in the morning when dewy.
Cleanly chop the plant material into small pieces and measure out 250 grams.
Combine chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate liquid from solids and discard solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for at least 2 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with swollen gums because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain. The tannins in elderflowers have a natural ability to constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in elderberries help to combat free radicals that can cause gum irritation and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment. By reducing inflammation and fighting off irritants, elder tinctures provide effective relief for swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Elder tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, which can cause swelling and inflammation in the gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, reduce swelling and pain in the gums by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and prevent infection in gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at full bloom for optimal potency and freshness.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently rinsing them with cool water and pat drying with paper towels.
Combine 100g of cleaned flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar ensuring complete immersion.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extract maximum bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids and storing the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its rich anti-inflammatory properties. The high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and silica in the tincture reduces inflammation and swelling, providing relief to irritated gum tissue. Additionally, its astringent properties help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and promoting healing. As a result, field horsetail tinctures can effectively soothe and calm swollen gums, making it an excellent natural remedy for gum health maintenance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail contains antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate swelling and pain in the gums.
Antioxidant: Horsetail's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause gum damage, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing inflammation.
Astringent: Horsetail's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which helps to alleviate swollen and irritated gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant contain high amounts of silica and antioxidants, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with swollen gums.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are underground stems that store nutrients and have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh field horsetail stems in the morning after dew has dried for maximum potency.
Clean and chop 2 ounces of horsetail stems into small pieces to release essential oils.
Combine chopped horsetail with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the mixture 2 inches deep.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking daily to extract active compounds slowly.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The soothing properties of chamomile also help to calm irritated gums and promote a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation. By using an herbal chamomile tincture, individuals can experience relief from swollen gums and maintain good oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by decreasing inflammation.
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help combat oral infections that can cause swollen gums.
Anti-anxiolytic property: Chamomile's anxiolytic effects can help reduce anxiety and stress-related jaw tension, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in gum tissue.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and other flavonoids, these delicate structures help soothe and calm inflamed gum tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh chamomile flowers from a trusted source, ensuring no contamination or pesticides.
Dry the harvested chamomile flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area using a food dehydrator or air circulation method.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with two parts vodka or everclear 70% alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cabinet for eight to twelve weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and tincture formation.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling the final product for future use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for swollen gums are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and flowers that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These tinctures help to alleviate swelling, reduce pain, and fight off infection in the gums. Examples of herbal tinctures that can help with swollen gums include peppermint, chamomile, and calendula. By using these tinctures, individuals suffering from swollen gums can experience relief from discomfort and improve their overall oral health, allowing them to enjoy a better quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal tinctures is to combine a few key botanicals in a single formula. Goldenrod tincture helps reduce inflammation, while echinacea supports immune function and promotes healing. Calendula tincture soothes and calms the gum tissue, reducing pain and discomfort. Mix 20-30 drops of each tincture with water and swish in your mouth several times daily to effectively address swollen gums naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal tinctures are various inflammatory conditions affecting the oral cavity. These include mouth ulcers, gum recession, toothaches, and abscesses. Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory compounds like calendula, myrrh, and chamomile help reduce pain and swelling, while antiseptic properties of garlic, tea tree oil, and goldenseal aid in preventing infection and promoting healing.
